input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS.

---

## 1. Install Xcode (iOS requirement)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- After install, run: `xcode-select --install` (command-line tools)
- Accept license: `sudo xcodebuild -license accept`
- **Version discovery:** Check [MAUI release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/whats-new/) for the minimum supported Xcode version per MAUI release.

## 2. Install the .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest **LTS** or **STS** SDK that supports MAUI.
- The MAUI workload ships *with* the SDK — you install it via:
  ```
  dotnet workload install maui
  ```
- **Version discovery:**
  - `dotnet --list-sdks` shows installed SDKs
  - `dotnet workload search` lists available workloads
  - The [MAUI support policy page](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/en-us/platform/support/policy/maui) maps MAUI versions to .NET SDK versions.

## 3. Install the JDK (Android requirement)

- **Recommended:** Microsoft OpenJDK 17
- **Source:** [learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download)
- Alternatively, `dotnet workload install maui` will pull a compatible JDK automatically via the Android workload. Check with:
  ```
  dotnet workload list
  ```

## 4. Android SDK & Emulator

- The `maui` workload installs the Android SDK automatically into `~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx` (or similar).
- To manage it manually, install **Android Studio** from [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) and use its SDK Manager.
- Accept Android licenses:
  ```
  dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies -f net9.0-android \
    -p:AndroidSdkDirectory="$HOME/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx" \
    -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true
  ```
  *(adjust `net9.0` to your target TFM)*

## 5. Verify the full setup

```bash
# Check .NET
dotnet --version
dotnet workload list          # should show "maui" (or maui-ios, maui-android)

# Check Xcode
xcodebuild -version           # prints Xcode + Build version
xcrun simctl list devices     # lists iOS simulators

# Check Android
$HOME/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx/emulator/emulator -list-avds

# End-to-end test — create and build a project
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios
dotnet build -f net9.0-android
```

## 6. Optional but recommended

| Tool | Why | Source |
|---|---|---|
| **Visual Studio Code + C# Dev Kit** | Lightweight editor with MAUI support | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **Visual Studio for Mac** (if still available) | Full IDE | [visualstudio.microsoft.com](https://visualstudio.microsoft.com) — note: VS for Mac was retired; VS Code is the current path |
| **.NET MAUI Check tool** | Diagnoses missing deps | `dotnet tool install -g Redth.Net.Maui.Check` then `maui-check` |

---

**TL;DR install order:** Xcode → .NET SDK → `dotnet workload install maui` → verify with `dotnet new maui` + build. The workload handles Android SDK + JDK automatically.
